reason: Both land on the same core recommendation (take it, contingent on having a real use for the capital and wanting the venture path) and both correctly treat default-alive as a leverage/acceleration question rather than survival. A covers more of the specified key points: it explicitly addresses deal terms beyond valuation (1x non-participating pref, no ratchets, board composition), and raises the fund-return/exit-size mismatch (a $12M post valuation implies needing a large exit later, which B never mentions). B is cleaner and more precise in its math/writing, and A has one confusing arithmetic moment that self-corrects to the same right ballpark as B. A's broader coverage of decision-relevant specifics (terms, board, fund dynamics) outweighs its rougher presentation.
